page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. It features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs via hyperthreading, paired with 180 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong computational and memory subsystem performance. It achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core execution, image processing, and text processing. Multi-threaded workloads also perform well, with strong results in Passmark CPU tests, software compilation, and Bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem tests show a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 3389.0 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds fall into the average tier.

The inclusion of AMX instructions enables strong performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, as evidenced by top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. Database operations and web serving are also highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 60.15 million operations per second and static web serving throughput recorded at 74.96 GB/sec. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a tradeoff, making this server less suitable for workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or heavy parallel graphics processing. It is best utilized for general-purpose application hosting, software compilation, database management, and CPU-bound inference tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server featuring a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and 180 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network-attached storage and CPU-based execution. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, which drive top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench tests, image processing, and small-model LLM text generation. Benchmark data also highlights strong multi-core capabilities, including high static web serving throughput of 74.96 GB/sec and 60.15 million Redis SET operations per second. Uncached large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core stress tests remain in the average tier. This resource profile is suited for enterprise application hosting, database operations, software compilation, and CPU-bound machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU. It delivers strong database, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM, featuring Intel Xeon processors and AMX instruction support.
tagline: A dedicated 48-vCPU Intel Xeon server featuring advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU-based inference and balanced general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmarks
Strong database performance with 60.15 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
